question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the cache amplification pattern: after seeding K slot keys for a contract, each new unique slot request triggers a findStorageBatch() with K+1 keys, meaning M new slot requests generate M*(K+M/2) total DB key lookups instead of M, creating superlinear DB load growth that overwhelms the database?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the cacheManagerSlotsPerContract.getCache(contractId.toString()) call inside a lambda passed to contractSlotsCache.get(), where the lambda is invoked on cache miss and creates a new CaffeineCache per contractId, and by cycling through many contractIds faster than the expireAfterAccess=5m TTL, accumulating thousands of live CaffeineCache objects in the JVM heap?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that the slotsPerContract cache has no maximumSize configured by default (only via CacheProperties.getSlotsPerContract()), and if the configuration is missing or misconfigured, the per-contract slot cache grows without bound, eventually exhausting JVM heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the ByteBuffer.wrap(key) call which does not copy the key array, and by passing a large byte array (e.g., Integer.MAX_VALUE bytes) as the slot key, causing the ByteBuffer to reference a massive backing array that is stored in the slotsPerContract cache, exhausting heap memory with a single request?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that contractSlotsCache stores CaffeineCache objects as values (not primitive values), and by filling the outer contractSlotsCache to its maximumSize=3000 limit with distinct contractIds, each holding a full slotsPerContract cache of 1500 entries, creating a worst-case memory footprint of 3000 * 1500 = 4.5 million cached ByteBuffer objects?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the Arrays.equals(slotKey, key) comparison inside the contractSlotValues loop at line 111, where if the DB returns a very large result set for findStorageBatch(), the O(N*32) byte comparison work per request under concurrent load causes CPU exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that when isEnableBatchContractSlotCaching() is false, findStorage() calls contractStateRepository.findStorage() directly with no rate limiting or circuit breaker, allowing an attacker to issue unlimited individual DB queries that each hit the contract_state table, saturating DB connections?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that the contractStateCache uses expireAfterWrite=2s, meaning that after 2 seconds all cached slot values expire simultaneously, and by seeding 25000 entries and waiting 2 seconds, trigger a thundering herd where all 25000 slots need to be re-fetched via findStorageBatch() at the same time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the interaction between the contractSlotsCache (expireAfterAccess=5m) and contractStateCache (expireAfterWrite=2s): slot keys remain in the slotsPerContract cache for 5 minutes but their values expire every 2 seconds, causing repeated full batch re-fetches of all accumulated slot keys every 2 seconds for the lifetime of the slotsPerContract cache?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that contractStateRepository.findStorageBatch() uses a SQL IN clause with a List<byte[]>, and by accumulating the maximum 1500 slot keys in the slotsPerContract cache, force the DB to process a prepared statement with 1500 bind parameters on every new slot lookup, causing DB statement parsing and execution overhead?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that the for loop at lines 96-101 iterates over all cachedSlotKeys and calls ((ByteBuffer) slot).array() on each, and by filling the cache with 1500 entries, each concurrent findStorageBatch() call performs 1500 ByteBuffer.array() calls and 1500 ByteBuffer.equals() comparisons, causing CPU saturation under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that contractSlotsCache.get(contractId, () -> cacheManagerSlotsPerContract.getCache(contractId.toString())) is not atomic with respect to the subsequent putIfAbsent and keySet operations, allowing two concurrent threads to both create sub-caches for the same contractId and both issue duplicate findStorageBatch() DB calls?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that the slotsPerContract cache is created via cacheManagerSlotsPerContract.getCache(contractId.toString()) which uses CaffeineCacheManager's dynamic cache creation, and by providing contractId strings that are very long or specially crafted, cause excessive string interning or memory allocation in the cache name registry?
